steps.title: Set equations equal and rearrange
steps.content:
Since both expressions equal $y$:
$$x + 9 = x^2 + 16x + 63$$
Rearranging to standard form by moving all terms to one side:
$$x^2 + 15x + 54 = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and find the greatest solution
steps.content:
Find two numbers that multiply to 54 and add to 15: these are 6 and 9.
$$(x + 6)(x + 9) = 0$$
This gives $x = -6$ or $x = -9$. The greatest of these two solutions is $-6$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
